question.text: Scenario: Meena Sharma, a 55-year-old retired school teacher from Jaipur, has mutual fund investments across 3 schemes of PQR Mutual Fund. She visits the Investor Service Centre (ISC) with the following requests: Request 1: She has recently moved from Jaipur to Udaipur and wants to update her address. She carries her new Aadhaar card showing the Udaipur address. Request 2: She has opened a new bank account with a different bank (Bank of Rajasthan) and wants all future redemption proceeds and IDCW payouts to go to this new account. She carries a cancelled cheque of the new account and her old bank's passbook. Request 3: She wants to add her daughter (Priya, age 28) and son (Rahul, age 32) as nominees with 60% and 40% allocation respectively. Currently, no nominee is registered. Request 4: She has not received her CAS for the last 6 months and wants to understand why and get it reissued. Request 5: She wants to know if she can consolidate her 3 folios — Folio 1 is in her single name, Folio 2 is joint with her husband (Meena + Suresh), and Folio 3 is in her single name with a different bank account. Question 4 of 5: For Request 4 (missing CAS), what is the most likely reason Meena hasn't received CAS for 6 months, and what should the RTA do?

answer.text: CAS cannot be reissued — Meena must wait for the next scheduled statement
CAS is only sent annually for retired investors
CAS generation has been suspended for her PAN due to an IT Department flag
If Meena had no transactions in those months, CAS would only be sent half-yearly; the RTA should check her transaction history and, if needed, generate an on-demand CAS

explanation.text: **The Logic:** CAS is sent monthly only if transactions occurred during the month. If Meena had no transactions for 6 months, she would receive CAS only half-yearly (January-June statement sent in July, July-December statement sent in January). The RTA should: (1) check her transaction history to confirm no transactions, (2) verify her email is correct and operational, (3) generate an on-demand CAS if she needs one immediately. **The Trap:** Non-receipt of monthly CAS doesn't indicate a problem if there were no transactions — it's the expected behavior. Many investors don't understand the monthly vs half-yearly CAS trigger. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A covers CAS frequency rules; Series II-B tests the RTA's troubleshooting process for CAS-related complaints. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Proactively explain the CAS frequency rule to clients — especially retired investors with lump sum investments and no SIPs, who may not transact for months. Also verify that their email isn't marking AMC/RTA emails as spam — a very common cause of 'missing' CAS.
